# Field Notes

> Design engineering knowledge, distilled.

This is a personal knowledge hub covering design systems, engineering principles, AI workflows, and platform architecture patterns.

## Principles & Frameworks

Core principles, delivery approach, synthesis and validation methodology.

- [Answer First](/principles/answer-first): Every document must have the conclusion in the first body section. Never bury the lead.
- [Compounding Engineering](/principles/compounding-engineering): Build guidance from real mistakes, not upfront speculation. Let documentation grow organically from actual needs.
- [Dissent is a Feature](/principles/dissent-as-feature): Structurally encourage and document dissent to prevent groupthink and preserve overruled concerns for future reference.
- [Principles & Frameworks](/principles): Core principles and operating frameworks distilled from real project work, designed to compound across every engagement.
- [Journey Mapping Template](/principles/journey-template): Reusable structure for mapping any part of a system from user need to validated outcome.
- [New Project Checklist](/principles/new-project-checklist): Bootstrap sequence using Field Notes and llms.txt to get a new project running.
- [Synthesis & Validation](/principles/synthesis-validation): Living document templates, sprint synthesis workflow, four-pillar validation, PAGE-TREE, and PR checklists.
- [Verification Over Instruction](/principles/verification-over-instruction): Give AI a way to verify its work rather than a long list of instructions. Success conditions beat specifications.

## Claude & AI Workflows

Context engineering, CLAUDE.md playbook, Claude Code setup, workflow architectures, skill-based automation.

- [Agent Efficiency Research](/claude/agent-efficiency): Reconciled findings from studies on AGENTS.md file impact.
- [Claude Code Setup](/claude/claude-code-setup): Practical guide for configuring Claude Code on a new project — CLAUDE.md, rules, hooks, and session management.
- [The CLAUDE.md Playbook](/claude/claude-md-playbook): How to write effective CLAUDE.md files — minimal, human-curated, and evolving.
- [Context Engineering](/claude/context-engineering): Strategies for curating the optimal set of tokens during LLM inference — a discipline that evolved from prompt engineering.
- [Dimensional Systems and DESIGN.md](/claude/dimensional-and-design-md): How a dimensional design system projects into the Google DESIGN.md spec, what is preserved, what is sidecarred, and the layered context stack that makes both legible to an agent.
- [Claude & AI Workflows](/claude): Patterns for working effectively with AI coding agents — from context engineering to workflow architectures.
- [Research to Validation Pathway](/claude/research-to-validation): A five-step pathway connecting prototypes to source research.
- [Skills Catalogue](/claude/skills-catalogue): A directory of available Claude skills with descriptions, trigger phrases, and usage guidance.
- [Skill-Based Automation](/claude/skills-patterns): Patterns for creating reusable Claude skills to automate repetitive workflows.
- [Workflow Architectures](/claude/workflow-architectures): Survey of proven Claude workflow patterns — from simple skill invocation to multi-agent orchestration.

## Platform Patterns

Monorepo architecture, data patterns, auth and RLS, bootstrapping.

- [ADR Template](/platform/adr-template): Architecture Decision Record format for documenting technical decisions with context, options, and consequences.
- [Audit-First Architecture](/platform/audit-first): Append-only event tables for complete state transition history.
- [Auth & Row-Level Security](/platform/auth-rls): JWT custom claims, RLS policies, and middleware route protection.
- [Bootstrap Commands](/platform/bootstrap-commands): Executable CLI sequences for each scaffolding phase, from empty directory to running CI.
- [Data Architecture Patterns](/platform/data-architecture): Splitting transactional and editorial data across purpose-built services.
- [Dependency Catalogue](/platform/dependency-catalogue): Curated package selections grouped by purpose, with alternatives and usage guidance.
- [Documentation Hub Pattern](/platform/documentation-hub): Multi-collection knowledge base with llms.txt generation for AI-consumable documentation.
- [Platform Patterns](/platform): Transferable architectural patterns for building platforms.
- [Monorepo Architecture](/platform/monorepo): pnpm workspaces + Turborepo for shared-code platform builds.
- [Scaffolding-First Approach](/platform/scaffolding-first): External services and AI context before any feature code.
- [Stack Decisions](/platform/stack-decisions): Record technology choices and their rationale before writing code.
- [Three-Application Model](/platform/three-app-model): Admin, public site, and wiki from a shared codebase — one platform, three faces.

## Design System

Multi-dimensional token model, composition rules, naming conventions, and constraints.

- [Accessibility Audit](/design-system/accessibility-audit): A bracket-based WCAG 2.2 AA audit framework for design system components with 30 rules and scored output.
- [Component Principles](/design-system/approaches/component-principles): Single-responsibility, composition, and API design for design system components.
- [Flat Architecture](/design-system/approaches/flat): Single-layer CSS custom properties for LLM-mediated workflows.
- [Approaches](/design-system/approaches): When to use the full layered architecture vs flat tokens.
- [Industry Research](/design-system/approaches/industry-research): How other design systems approach the same problems we solve with the dimensional model — detailed analysis by project, with dates and URLs for future rescanning
- [Layered Architecture](/design-system/approaches/layered): The full four-layer token chain for enterprise and multi-brand systems.
- [Why Dimensional](/design-system/approaches/why-dimensional): A plain-language explanation of how the dimensional model compares to typical design systems — and why it works better.
- [Why This Works](/design-system/approaches/why-this-works): Rules and reasoning for the dimensional model — written so an LLM understands the benefits well enough to apply them when building components
- [Component API](/design-system/component-api): How to define the consumer-facing contract of a design system component — inputs, types, defaults, and slots.
- [Component Scaffolding](/design-system/component-scaffolding): The workflow for scaffolding a new design system component — from dimension decisions through file generation to visual iteration.
- [Composition](/design-system/composition): The checklist and rules for composing components with the dimensional model.
- [Sentiment + Emphasis](/design-system/composition/sentiment-emphasis): The relationship between what a component says and how loudly it says it.
- [Spacing & Gestalt](/design-system/composition/spacing-gestalt): Spatial distance encodes semantic distance — the three spacing tiers.
- [Worked Examples](/design-system/composition/worked-examples): Real-world scenarios showing how to apply the dimensional model.
- [Concepts Manifest](/design-system/concepts-manifest): The single source of truth for valid dimension values across the design system.
- [Accessibility](/design-system/constraints/accessibility): Touch targets, focus rings, contrast, and reduced motion requirements.
- [Dos and Don'ts](/design-system/constraints/dos-and-donts): Practical rules for using tokens correctly in components and layouts.
- [Constraints & Rules](/design-system/constraints): Guardrails for working with the token system.
- [CSS Authoring Rules](/design-system/css-authoring-rules): Opinionated rules for writing component stylesheets in a design system — section order, token consumption, and seven non-negotiables.
- [Figma Documentation Rules](/design-system/figma-documentation-rules): Best practices for documenting design tokens and components inside Figma files.
- [HTML Semantics](/design-system/html-semantics): Rules for choosing native HTML elements and ARIA attributes in design system components.
- [Token Approach Guide](/design-system): A living methodology for multi-dimensional design token systems.
- [Color Scale](/design-system/model/color-scale): The 12-step color ramp (50–1000) and the UI role of each step.
- [Density — why it is not a dimension](/design-system/model/density-note): Density was considered as a sixth dimension in the bysixteen model. This note documents why it was not adopted and how Size covers the same use cases.
- [Emphasis](/design-system/model/emphasis): How loudly a component communicates — the visual weight and intensity of its signal.
- [The Dimensional Model](/design-system/model): Every component is the product of up to five independent, orthogonal dimensions.
- [Sentiment](/design-system/model/sentiment): What a component communicates — its semantic intent, not its visual appearance.
- [Size](/design-system/model/size): The physical footprint of a component — height, touch target, and padding scale.
- [State](/design-system/model/state): The interactive condition of a component — always runtime, never a composition decision.
- [Structure](/design-system/model/structure): The fixed anatomical skeleton of a component — internal padding, gap, fixed heights.
- [Theme](/design-system/model/theme): The color scheme dimension — light and dark modes.
- [Emphasis Scale](/design-system/naming/emphasis-scale): The weaker → weak → standard → strong naming convention for visual weight.
- [Naming Conventions](/design-system/naming): How tokens are named across the primitive and semantic layers.
- [Primitive Naming](/design-system/naming/primitives): The --{category}-{name} pattern for raw token values.
- [Semantic Naming](/design-system/naming/semantics): The --{element}-{qualifier} pattern for purpose-driven tokens.
- [PRISM — Figma MCP Bridge](/design-system/prism): Interface contract for PRISM (prism-figma-mcp v0.2.0), a 71-tool bridge between local design-token tooling and Figma via the Model Context Protocol.
- [Storybook Documentation Rules](/design-system/storybook-documentation-rules): Best practices for documenting design systems in Storybook with consistent structure and components.
- [Testing Strategy](/design-system/testing-strategy): A three-layer testing approach for design system components — unit, accessibility, and visual regression.
- [Token Audit](/design-system/token-audit): How to audit component tokens for cascade integrity, hardcoded values, and structural correctness.
- [Component Slots](/design-system/token-chain/component-slots): Layer 3 — named openings inside a component's variable collections.
- [The Token Chain](/design-system/token-chain): All visual values flow through a strict four-layer resolution chain.
- [Primitives](/design-system/token-chain/primitives): Layer 1 — raw values that are theme-aware but never used directly in components.
- [Resolver](/design-system/token-chain/resolver): Layer 4 — runtime engine that resolves multi-dimensional token combinations.
- [Semantics](/design-system/token-chain/semantics): Layer 2 — purpose-driven aliases that describe what a value means, not its raw appearance.
- [Token Transform Pipeline](/design-system/token-transform-pipeline): End-to-end pipeline from designer anchor choices through generation, layering, and output to CSS custom properties.
- [Figma Sync Scripts](/design-system/tooling/figma-sync): Push and pull design tokens between local tooling and Figma via the PRISM MCP bridge.
- [Tooling](/design-system/tooling): Scripts, bridges, and automation that connect the design system to external tools.
